title: Economic Security and Technology
gist: CSIS's Economic Security and Technology hub flags software (not drones) as the decisive military variable, China's countermove to Europe's carbon border tax, and India-Africa EV cooperation as the department's featured strategic bets.

insights.claim: CSIS argues that software, not drones, will decide the next war, reframing autonomy as a code-and-integration problem rather than a hardware race.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: Shifts defense-tech investment thesis toward autonomy software and integration layers over airframe manufacturers.
insights.locator: Defining Autonomy: Why Software, Not Drones

insights.claim: CSIS analysts examine the Maven Smart System, the U.S. military's AI-enabled targeting and data-fusion platform, as a live case of operational military AI.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: Maven is the reference deployment for defense AI contractors; understanding it maps the government AI procurement stack.

insights.claim: China is crafting a 'new carbon order' in response to Europe's Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM), positioning its own carbon-pricing and trade response.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: CBAM is reshaping global carbon-intensive trade flows; China's counter-design affects steel, aluminum, and cement exporters.
insights.locator: The New Carbon Order: China's Response

insights.claim: CSIS advocates accelerating the EV transition in the Global South through an India-Africa partnership, linking two emerging markets in a single mobility supply chain.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: India-Africa EV linkage is a corridor to watch for battery and vehicle demand outside the China-West axis.
insights.locator: Accelerating the EV Transition in the Global South

insights.claim: A CSIS commission is examining U.S. cyber force generation, signaling institutional concern that the military's cyber manpower model is inadequate.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: Cyber force-generation reform points to sustained federal demand for cyber talent and training contractors.
insights.locator: CSIS Commission on U.S. Cyber Force Generation
